mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns across multiple quarters, invalidating the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, negating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal income confirms broad-based demand strength, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields a negative or zero rank IC over 20-day horizons during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicate supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 45% over a 3-year sample.
